title: Spectrophotometric Determination of Mo(Vl) with Benzohydroxamic acid
author: Y. K. AGRAWAL
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of  Chemistry, Govt. Polytechnic College. Ujjain (M.P.), India 

  Manuscript received 6 April 1976: accepted 10 November 1976  

 A rapid method for the simultaneous solvent extraction and spectrophotometric determination of  micro amounts of  hexavalent molybdenum with benzohydroxamic acid  is described. The intense yellow-greenish coloured complex thus extracted from hexanol at  p H 2.0 absorbs at around 370 nm. A clear cut separation from many commonly occurring metal ions is easily accomplished. Effect of acidity, reagent concentration and diverse ions on the visible absorption of the extracted complex have also been investigated.
